districts.title: District Intelligence
districts.subtitle: Neighborhood analytics and market yields across the 17 administrative districts of Banadir.
districts.expand_desc: 11 more districts currently undergoing geospatial analysis and yield auditing for Q3 reports.

Abdiaziz.desc: Coastal district famous for Liido Beach. Prime location for tourism and high-end apartments.

Bondhere.desc: Centrally located with many government offices and quiet residential streets.

Daynile.desc: Large expanding district on the outskirts. High potential for industrial and suburb development.

Dharkenley.desc: Densely populated residential district in the south-west. Stable rental demand.

Hamar Jajab.desc: Historic district near the Mogadishu Port. Strategic for logistics and trade.

Hamar Weyne.desc: The historic old city center. Narrow streets with high commercial density.

Heliwa.desc: North-eastern district with a mix of residential and agricultural zones.

Hodan.desc: The commercial heart of modern Mogadishu. High demand for office spaces and luxury villas.

Howlwadag.desc: Home to the massive Bakaara Market. The engine of Somalia's informal economy.

Karan.desc: Northern coastal district known for its clean air and expanding residential estates.

Shangani.desc: One of the oldest districts, characterized by unique architecture and seaside views.

Shibis.desc: A well-established residential district with central access to main city arteries.

Waberi.desc: Strategic district housing the Airport and many international organizations.

Wadajir.desc: Vibrant and diverse district including the famous KM4 intersection.

Wardhigley.desc: Administrative center of the city, located near the Presidential Palace.

Yaqshid.desc: Largest district by population. Massive scale for residential and retail management.

Kahda.desc: Mogadishu's newest administrative district. A rapidly growing residential frontier.
